leg: B — Word → clone, styles (spec §8.3 step 2, slice 3 Task 13.2)
method: Authored IN REAL Word via the AppleScript object model: make new document; 8 paragraphs via set content of text object; paragraph styles via enum tokens (style heading1 / heading3 / title / subtitle — all worked first try) and name strings ('Intense Quote', 'No Spacing'); character styles Strong + Subtle Emphasis applied to single words — the direct word-range set (set style of (words 2 thru 2 of text object of paragraph N of d) to "Strong") raised -1728 on both attempts, the WORKING form is create range d start/end from start/end of content of the word specifier, then set style of <range> to "Strong" (attempt 2 of 3; new oracle knowledge). Word itself saved to tests/fixtures/oracle-word-s3-styles.docx (sandbox-safe repo path); closed by the NEW basename via name-verified ordinal. Clone import: headless --probe-out evalfile inlining the file as base64 → await WC.PM.openDocx(bytes) → 400 ms settle → per-paragraph styleId walk + textStyle-mark walk + getState().block with the caret placed in the Heading 1 paragraph.
wordAuthoredReadback: In-Word verification before save: 1:Heading 1 | 2:Heading 3 | 3:Title | 4:Subtitle | 5:Intense Quote | 6:No Spacing | 7:Normal | 8:Normal; p7 word2 [strong ] style=Strong; p8 word2 [subtle ] style=Subtle Emphasis (word ranges include the trailing space — oracle quirk #12).
wordFileXml: word/document.xml: w:pStyle Heading1, Heading3, Title, Subtitle, IntenseQuote, NoSpacing + w:rStyle Strong, SubtleEmphasis — exactly the ids our STYLE_NAME_TO_ID vocabulary speaks.

rows.wordApplied: (paragraph left Normal; word 2 → Strong char style via create range)
(paragraph left Normal; word 2 → Subtle Emphasis char style via create range)

rows.probe: gallery state with caret in the Heading 1 paragraph

verdict: PASS 9/9 — every Word-authored paragraph style resolves to the expected styleId in the PM model, both character styles arrive as textStyle marks with the right styleId, and the ribbon-gallery state reports the resolved display name.

mintedDefaultsDiff.method: unzip -p tests/fixtures/oracle-word-s3-styles.docx word/styles.xml; Word's emitted w:style definitions for NoSpacing / Strong / Emphasis / SubtleEmphasis diffed against the minted defaults in src/renderer/core/superdoc-fork/core/super-converter/exporter-docx-defs.js. Context that matters: THIS machine's Word template emits an EMPTY w:pPrDefault and a bare Normal (no w:pPr at all) — Normal already has 0 space-after and single line spacing, identical to the clone export's docDefaults (modulo w:lang en-TR vs en-US, non-visual).

rows.ourMinted: same name/uiPriority/qFormat + explicit w:pPr > w:spacing w:after=0 w:line=240 w:lineRule=auto
rows.verdict: NOT MATERIAL — Word omitted the spacing block because this template's Normal/docDefaults already resolve to after=0/line=240(single), so the inherited values equal our explicit ones; both files render identically. (Word only writes the style's delta against ITS template.)

rows.wordEmitted: character, basedOn DefaultParagraphFont, uiPriority 22, qFormat, rPr: w:b + w:bCs
not emitted by Word (latent) — unused in the document, Word writes only in-use styles + defaults
rows.verdict: NO ORACLE SIGNAL — relying on the styles Word did emit; our i/iCs shape mirrors the verified Strong/SubtleEmphasis pattern

rows.wordEmitted: character, basedOn DefaultParagraphFont, uiPriority 19, qFormat, rPr: w:i + w:iCs + w:color w:val=404040 w:themeColor=text1 w:themeTint=BF
rows.ourMinted: w:i + w:iCs + w:color w:val=595959 w:themeColor=text1 w:themeTint=A6
rows.verdict: MATERIAL DIFFERENCE — Word 16.77.1 mints a DARKER gray (404040, themeTint BF) than our 595959/A6 (the older Office-template value). Italic shape matches; only the color tint differs. Flagged for the controller: amend exporter-docx-defs.js SubtleEmphasis to 404040/text1/BF to match live Word, or record as an accepted template-era deviation.

pidSafety: Authoring on a fresh make-new document; save as to the repo path renamed the doc, closed by the NEW basename via name-verified ordinal (by-name access flickered -1728 once, quirk #26); document count 0 verified after the leg; Word never quit.
